Honeywell Retirement Benefits Help

Have you or a spouse retired from Honeywell, Allied Signal, Signal, UOP, Bendix, Prestone, Allied Affiliated, Protection Services, Honeywell Pension, Westinghouse Security Electronics, or another Honeywell-affiliated company?

If you or a spouse are retired and receiving monthly retirement benefits from the company, you may have legal rights to challenge the amount of your monthly benefit.

The Honeywell Retirement Plans may be underpaying retiree benefits.

If you or your spouse are receiving a monthly retirement benefit through Honeywell, Allied Signal, Signal, UOP, Bendix, Prestone, Allied Affiliated, Protection Services, Honeywell Pension, Westinghouse Security Electronics, or another Honeywell-affiliated company, know your rights. See if you may be entitled to additional retirement benefits today at no cost.

Speak with the legal team at no cost today.

Free Case Review

Submit your information for a free case review, and an attorney will contact you shortly to discuss your options.

Privacy PolicyTerms and Conditions

Attorney Advertising – This website may be construed as legal advertising. Martin & Bonnett, PLLC, 4647 N. 32nd Street, Suite 185, Phoenix, Arizona 85018. (602) 240-6900. Attorneys Susan Martin and Jennifer Kroll.

This website is for educational and informational purposes only and it does not constitute legal advice. Any information contained on this website should not be relied upon without consulting with an attorney licensed to practice in the jurisdiction in which your matter arises. Laws and legal requirements are subject to revision and interpretation. We make no representation, warranty or claim that the information contained on this website is current. We are not responsible for any errors or omissions in the resources or information available at or from this website. Any results portrayed in this advertisement are dependent upon the facts and law applicable to each particular case, and the results will differ based on the particular facts and law applicable in each case. Nothing contained on this website constitutes a guarantee, warranty or prediction regarding the outcome of a specific legal matter. No representation is made that the quality of the legal services to be performed is greater than the quality of legal services performed by other lawyers. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome. Images may be fictionalized.

No attorney-client relationship is formed by your use of this website or by any communication you send or receive through this site. The content and features of this website, including means to submit a question or information, do not constitute an offer to represent you. Transmission of information via this website is not intended to create, and its receipt will not create, an attorney-client relationship between a lawyer and the reader. You are not formally represented by a law firm unless and until a contract of representation is signed by you and the law firm. We reserve the right to represent an adverse party until a contract of representation is signed by you and our firm. Please do not send any confidential information, or any other information that you would expect to be maintained in confidence, unless and until such time, if any, as the Martin & Bonnett, PLLC has acknowledged the creation of an attorney-client relationship with you.

By clicking submit, you agree to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy and that we may contact you using any of the information that you have provided. By 'accepting', I agree to have a lawyer contact me. I affirm that I am 18 years or older and I consent to receiving automated marketing texts/calls from this website and associated entities, and I understand that this authorization overrides any previous registrations on a federal or state DO NOT CALL registry. Martin & Bonnett, PLLC undertakes no obligation to respond to unsolicited communications, including email, and has no duty to keep confidential any information transmitted in any unsolicited communication. A lawyer may contact you regarding your case based on the information submitted to discuss potential representation, but we cannot guarantee a response to your inquiry. You should continue to seek legal assistance while we review your potential claim. No one should act, or refrain from acting, on the basis of any information communicated by this website without obtaining legal or other professional advice.

Martin & Bonnett, PLLC may refer you to other attorneys or non-lawyer professionals under a referral agreement. These agreements are not exclusive, meaning both our firm and the other professionals are free to refer clients to others. If a referral is recommended or requested, we will inform you of any existing referral agreement and clarify that you are under no obligation to engage the referred professional. You are always free to seek advice from any other professional of your choosing.
